metadata.description: Essential Chinese vocabulary for shopping, including store items, colors, sizes, and money

content.name: store
content.side_f: I'm going to the store to buy things.
content.side_g: 商店 is a generic 'shop/store'; for bigger venues use 超市 (supermarket) or 百货商店 (department store). Often preceded by 去 or 在. Neutral register.

content.name: buy
content.side_f: What do you want to buy?
content.side_g: 买 mǎi (buy) and 卖 mài (sell) differ only in tone—classic minimal pair to drill. Takes a direct object; resultative 买到 means 'manage to buy'. Neutral register.

content.name: things
content.side_f: I want to buy a few things.
content.side_g: 东西 is pronounced dōngxi (neutral tone on 西) when meaning 'things'; dōngxī (full tone) means 'east–west' directions. Can also colloquially refer to people, usually pejoratively (e.g., 坏东西).

content.name: shop_assistant
content.side_f: The salesclerk is very warm/attentive.
content.side_g: 售货员 is the textbook word; in modern usage 店员 diànyuán or 服务员 fúwùyuán are more common. 员 -yuán is a productive suffix meaning 'staff member' (e.g., 服务员, 运动员).

content.name: clothes
content.side_f: This piece of clothing is very nice-looking.
content.side_g: 衣服 is a mass/generic term; 服 is neutral tone here. Use 件 as measure word. Note 衣服 covers tops and general clothing, while 裤子 (pants) takes 条.

content.name: measure_word_shirt
content.side_a: Measure word for shirts, dresses, jackets, coats, etc.
content.side_f: I bought two shirts.
content.side_g: 件 jiàn is the measure word for upper-body garments (shirts, jackets, coats) and also abstract 'items' like 事 (matters) and 礼物 (gifts). Does not fit pants or skirts.

content.name: shirt
content.side_e: Zhè jiàn chènshān shì hóng de.
content.side_f: This shirt is red.
content.side_g: 衬衫 specifically means a button-up/dress shirt; 'T-shirt' is T恤 (T-xù). Pair with 件. Often precedes colors via 的 structure: 红的衬衫 or 衬衫是红的.

content.name: color
content.side_f: What color do you like?
content.side_g: 颜色 is the abstract noun 'color'; individual colors (红, 黄, 蓝) are adjectives that typically take 色 suffix (红色, 黄色) when used as nouns. Question pattern: 什么颜色 is the go-to.

content.name: yellow
content.side_f: I like yellow shirts.
content.side_g: 黄 as adjective attaches directly (黄衬衫) or with 色的 (黄色的衬衫). Culturally, 黄 can connote 'smutty/obscene' in 黄色电影—rely on context. Classical association with the emperor.

content.name: red
content.side_e: Zhè tiáo kùzi tài hóng le.
content.side_f: These pants are too red.
content.side_g: 红 is auspicious in Chinese culture (weddings, New Year) and also means 'popular/famous' (他很红 = he's famous). As attributive adjective add 的 when polysyllabic context: 红色的.

content.name: wear
content.side_e: Tā jīntiān chuānle yí jiàn hóng chènshān.
content.side_f: She's wearing a red shirt today.
content.side_g: 穿 is for items you put your body into/through: clothes, pants, shoes, socks. Accessories (hats, glasses, watches, scarves) take 戴 dài instead. Past/completed state often uses 穿着 chuānzhe.

content.name: measure_word_pants
content.side_a: Measure word for pants and long, thin objects
content.side_e: Wǒ xiǎng mǎi yì tiáo kùzi.
content.side_f: I want to buy a pair of pants.
content.side_g: 条 tiáo is the measure word for long, narrow things: pants, skirts, rivers, roads, fish, dogs, ties, news (新闻). Contrasts with 件 for upper garments.

content.name: pants
content.side_e: Zhè tiáo kùzi bù cháng yě bù duǎn.
content.side_f: These pants are neither long nor short.
content.side_g: 裤子 always takes 条, never 件. 子 is neutral tone. Shorts = 短裤; jeans = 牛仔裤 niúzǎikù. Singular form refers to one pair.

content.name: size
content.side_f: What size do you wear? (Literally: you wear how-big a size?)
content.side_g: 号 hào means 'number/size' and also 'date' (五月三号). For clothing sizes, ask 多大号 or 几号. Don't confuse with 大小 dàxiǎo which is abstract 'size' as a noun.

content.name: medium
content.side_f: I wear size medium.
content.side_g: 中 zhōng means 'medium/middle'; sizes S/M/L are 小号/中号/大号. 中 also appears in 中国 (Middle Kingdom), 中午 (noon). When used as verb 'to hit' it's pronounced zhòng (4th tone).

content.name: cheap
content.side_f: This piece of clothing is very cheap.
content.side_g: 便宜 piányi (neutral tone on 宜) is the everyday word—neutral connotation, no 'cheap quality' implication. Opposite is 贵 guì. Note 便 here is pián, not biàn (as in 方便 fāngbiàn).

content.name: if
content.side_e: Rúguǒ piányi de huà, wǒ jiù mǎi.
content.side_f: If it's cheap, then I'll buy it. (Literally: if-cheap-'s speech, I then buy.)
content.side_g: 如果...的话 is a paired conditional frame; the 的话 tag is optional and softens the tone. The second clause usually takes 就 jiù. Colloquially 要是 yàoshi...的话 is more casual.

content.name: length
content.side_e: Zhè tiáo kùzi de chángduǎn zěnmeyàng?
content.side_f: How is the length of these pants? (Literally: long-short)
content.side_g: 长短 is an antonym-compound noun meaning 'length' (lit. long-short). Similar pattern: 大小 (size), 多少 (amount), 高矮 (height), 好坏 (quality). Abstract/measurable dimension noun.

content.name: long
content.side_f: These pants are a bit long.
content.side_g: 长 has two readings: cháng (long, adj.) and zhǎng (to grow; elder, as in 长大, 校长). Context and grammar distinguish them. Antonym 短 duǎn.

content.name: short
content.side_e: Chènshān tài duǎn le, bù héshì.
content.side_f: The shirt is too short, it doesn't fit.
content.side_g: 短 duǎn is for physical length and time spans (短时间). For 'short in height' of a person, use 矮 ǎi, not 短. Pairs with 长 cháng.

content.name: suitable
content.side_f: This shirt fits/suits well.
content.side_g: 合适 covers both 'fits (size)' and 'suitable (appropriate)'. Adjective only—cannot take an object directly. For 'suitable for X' use 对 X 合适 or 适合 shìhé (which is a verb and CAN take objects: 适合你).

content.name: try
content.side_f: Can I try it on?
content.side_g: 试 often reduplicates as 试试 or combines with 一下 to soften ('give it a try'). For trying on clothes specifically, 试穿 shìchuān is the compound. Also 考试 (to test/exam) and 试试看 (try and see).

content.name: need_not
content.side_e: Búyòng shì le, wǒ zhīdào héshì.
content.side_f: No need to try, I know it fits. (Literally: not-use)
content.side_g: 不用 = 'need not / no need to', a polite refusal equivalent to English 'don't bother'. 不 becomes bú (2nd tone) before 4th-tone 用. Compare 不必 búbì (more formal) and 别 bié (don't, prohibitive).

content.name: altogether
content.side_f: How much altogether?
content.side_g: 一共 yígòng sums up totals; often paired with 多少钱 in shopping. Similar: 总共 zǒnggòng (more formal), 一块儿 (together, spatial).

content.name: how_much
content.side_f: How much is this piece of clothing?
content.side_g: 多少 works for any quantity (no upper bound) and for prices/phone numbers; 几 jǐ is for small expected numbers (<10) and requires a measure word. 多少 often omits the measure word.

content.name: money
content.side_f: I don't have money.
content.side_g: 钱 is the generic noun 'money'; cash = 现金 xiànjīn, change = 零钱 língqián. Common compounds: 花钱 (spend), 赚钱/挣钱 (earn), 找钱 (give change), 付钱 (pay).

content.name: kuai
content.side_a: measure word for Chinese monetary unit)
content.side_e: Zhè jiàn chènshān wǔshí kuài qián.
content.side_f: This shirt is fifty kuai.
content.side_g: 块 kuài is the colloquial spoken measure word for yuan 元 (the formal/written form). Use 块 when speaking, 元 on receipts/formal. Also means 'piece/chunk' (一块蛋糕). Omit 钱 when followed by 毛/分.

content.name: mao
content.side_a: measure word for 1/10 of a kuai, dime
content.side_f: Three kuai five mao. (i.e., 3.5 yuan)
content.side_g: 毛 máo = 1/10 yuan (jiao 角 in formal writing). In spoken prices, when 毛 is the last unit, 钱 is optional and the 毛 itself may be dropped: 三块五 = 3.50. Also means 'fur/hair' (毛发).

content.name: fen
content.side_a: measure word for 1/100 of a kuai, cent)
content.side_e: Yí kuài èr máo wǔ fēn.
content.side_f: One kuai, two mao, five fen. (1.25 yuan)
content.side_g: 分 fēn = 1/100 yuan (cent); rarely used today due to inflation. 分 also means 'minute' (时间), 'point/score' (考试), and as verb 'to divide'. Formal written equivalent for currency is 分.

content.name: hundred
content.side_f: One hundred kuai altogether.
content.side_g: 百 always needs 一 before it in counting (一百, not just 百). For 100+ numbers: 一百零五 (105, with 零 for the zero slot). Tone change: 一 is 4th tone before 3rd-tone 百 → yìbǎi.

content.name: give_change
content.side_f: (Here's) five kuai change for you. (Literally: find you five kuai money)
content.side_g: 找 as 'give change' is a verb-object compound where 找 literally means 'seek/find'. The same character 找 also means 'to look for' (我找你 = I'm looking for you). Pattern: 找 + [person] + [amount] 钱.

lines.a: 对不起，这双鞋太小了。能不能换一双？
lines.b: Duìbuqǐ, zhè shuāng xié tài xiǎo le. Néng bu néng huàn yì shuāng?
lines.c: Excuse me, this pair of shoes is too small. Can I exchange them?

lines.a: 没问题。您看，这双怎么样？
lines.b: Méi wèntí. Nín kàn, zhè shuāng zěnmeyàng?
lines.c: No problem. Please take a look—how about this pair?

lines.a: 也不行，这双跟那双一样大。
lines.b: Yě bù xíng, zhè shuāng gēn nà shuāng yíyàng dà.
lines.c: Still not okay; this pair is the same size as that pair.

lines.a: 那这双黑的呢？
lines.b: Nà zhè shuāng hēi de ne?
lines.c: Then how about this black pair?

lines.a: 这双鞋虽然大小合适，可是颜色不好。有没有咖啡色的？
lines.b: Zhè shuāng xié suīrán dàxiǎo héshì, kěshì yánsè bù hǎo. Yǒu méiyǒu kāfēisè de?
lines.c: Although this pair fits, the color isn't good. Do you have brown ones?

lines.a: 对不起，这种鞋只有黑的。
lines.b: Duìbuqǐ, zhè zhǒng xié zhǐ yǒu hēi de.
lines.c: Sorry, this style only comes in black.

lines.a: 这双鞋样子挺好的……就是它吧。你们这儿可以刷卡吗？
lines.b: Zhè shuāng xié yàngzi tǐng hǎo de... jiù shì tā ba. Nǐmen zhèr kěyǐ shuā kǎ ma?
lines.c: This pair looks pretty good... I'll take it. Can I pay by card?

lines.a: 对不起，我们不收信用卡。不过，这双的钱跟那双一样，您不用再付钱了。
lines.b: Duìbuqǐ, wǒmen bù shōu xìnyòngkǎ. Búguò, zhè shuāng de qián gēn nà shuāng yíyàng, nín búyòng zài fù qián le.
lines.c: Sorry, we don't accept credit cards. However, this pair costs the same as that pair, so you don't need to pay again.

lines.a: 我在商店要买东西，看看衣服。
lines.b: Wǒ zài shāngdiàn yào mǎi dōngxi, kànkan yīfu.
lines.c: I'm at the store to buy some things, taking a look at clothes.

lines.a: 我找售货员问一下：这件衬衫什么颜色？
lines.b: Wǒ zhǎo shòuhuòyuán wèn yíxià: zhè jiàn chènshān shénme yánsè?
lines.c: I'll find a shop assistant and ask: what color is this shirt?

lines.a: 有黄色的和红色的。您可以试试。
lines.b: Yǒu huángsè de hé hóngsè de. Nín kěyǐ shìshi.
lines.c: We have yellow and red. You can try them on.

lines.a: 我试这件中号的，看看合不合适。
lines.b: Wǒ shì zhè jiàn zhōng hào de, kànkan hé bu héshì.
lines.c: I'll try this medium one and see if it fits.

lines.a: 穿穿看，颜色可以，就是有点儿长，不要太短。
lines.b: Chuān chuān kàn, yánsè kěyǐ, jiù shì yǒudiǎnr cháng, búyào tài duǎn.
lines.c: Trying it on—the color is fine, but the length isn't quite right.

lines.a: 这条裤子呢？如果便宜一点儿的话，我就买。
lines.b: Zhè tiáo kùzi ne? Rúguǒ piányi yìdiǎnr de huà, wǒ jiù mǎi.
lines.c: How about these pants? If they're a bit cheaper, I'll buy them.

lines.a: 这条很便宜，样子也挺好。您再试试长短。
lines.b: Zhè tiáo hěn piányi, yàngzi yě tǐng hǎo. Nín zài shìshi chángduǎn.
lines.c: These are very cheap, and the style is quite good. Please try the length again.

lines.a: 现在大小合适了。我就要它吧。
lines.b: Xiànzài dàxiǎo héshì le. Wǒ jiù yào tā ba.
lines.c: Now the size is right. I'll take it.

lines.a: 这件和那件衣服一共多少钱？
lines.b: Zhè jiàn hé nà jiàn yīfu yígòng duōshao qián?
lines.c: How much are this one and that one altogether?

lines.a: 一共一百二十块五毛三分。
lines.b: Yígòng yì bǎi èrshí kuài wǔ máo sān fēn.
lines.c: Altogether it's 120 yuan and 53 cents.

lines.a: 我用卡付，可以刷卡吗？
lines.b: Wǒ yòng kǎ fù, kěyǐ shuā kǎ ma?
lines.c: I'll pay with a card—can I swipe it?

lines.a: 对不起，我们不收信用卡。请付钱。
lines.b: Duìbuqǐ, wǒmen bù shōu xìnyòngkǎ. Qǐng fù qián.
lines.c: Sorry, we don't accept credit cards. Please pay.

lines.a: 好，我给您二百块。
lines.b: Hǎo, wǒ gěi nín èr bǎi kuài.
lines.c: Okay, here are 200 yuan.

lines.a: 我找您七十九块四毛七分钱。收好，谢谢。
lines.b: Wǒ zhǎo nín qīshí jiǔ kuài sì máo qī fēn qián. Shōu hǎo, xièxie.
lines.c: I'll give you 79 yuan and 47 cents in change. Please keep it safe—thanks.
